what_input_feature: Same program-side condition (non-matrix-shaper v4+ perceptual/saturation profile); the distinguishing input feature is a profile that drives cmsIsMatrixShaper to FALSE, with the false-side basic-block edge becoming new coverage when indexed by the deep call context (cmsDetectBlackPoint reached via ComputeConversion/DefaultICCintents).
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winner seed (the naive_ctx-stored false-branch seed) carries non-RGB signature bytes ('REB' at offsets 0x10-0x12) and reaches the blocker; the false-branch edge is recorded at a context-specific map slot, so a partial advance toward the non-matrix-shaper class registers as new coverage and is preserved.
why_loser_doesnt: naive records the cmsIsMatrixShaper edge in a single context-agnostic map slot, so once the matrix-shaper true branch (line 219) is covered it gets no new-coverage credit for inputs edging toward the false side, and stays blocked on the 'RGB' true-branch profile.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the (call-context, edge) pair for the false side of cmsIsMatrixShaper is a distinct coverage bucket; this finer granularity rewards intermediate inputs that naive discards, letting the campaign retain and extend toward the non-matrix-shaper profile.

what_input_feature: Same valid-but-disallowed color_type/bit_depth combination at offsets 0x18/0x19 reaching L2636 TRUE; no extra byte requirement beyond naive's.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves on all 10 trials; its context-indexed edge map records OSS_FUZZ_png_check_IHDR edges under distinct call contexts, retaining more intermediate IHDR-parsing inputs and preserving the corpus diversity that eventually produces the forbidden combination.
why_loser_doesnt: naive collapses all call paths into bare edges, so context-distinct IHDR explorations dedup to the same bucket and are discarded; it stays blocked 9/10 trials and never assembles the combination.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same IHDR-validation edge is new coverage under a new call context; this finer feedback granularity preserves more partial inputs and increases the chance of stumbling onto the disallowed color_type/bit_depth bytes.

what_input_feature: A parsed numeric-string field (the FP-number scanner walks string[i] through a state machine) that feeds png_check_fp_number a character sequence which puts the parser in the PNG_FP_INTEGER state with PNG_FP_SAW_DOT already set, then supplies a second '.' (byte 0x2e) so that case PNG_FP_INTEGER + PNG_FP_SAW_DOT is hit while (state & PNG_FP_SAW_DOT) != 0 — the 'two dots' true side at L2751.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all 10/10 trials with 30 hits on the branch and reaches the L2752 goto-end true arm ([W] 2752); per-role counts show the winner-resolving set takes the true side (L2751 W T=28 F=28) whereas no path to a second dot is retained by the loser. The CtxHook splits the edge map by call context, so each distinct caller that drives the FP-number scanner records the same parser edges at separate map slots; this keeps intermediate FP-parser progress (sign/digit/first-dot states) as separately-rewarded corpus entries, so the rare second-dot continuation is reachable from a retained mid-parse seed rather than requiring it to be found in one shot.
why_loser_doesnt: plain naive blocks all 10/10 trials with 0 hits on the branch; its per-role counts show the loser never takes the L2751 true side (L T=0 F=21) — it explores PNG_FP_FRACTION arms (L2784/L2788 fire only in L) but never re-enters the PNG_FP_INTEGER+SAW_DOT case with SAW_DOT already set. Without context-indexed edges, naive collapses every call path through png_check_fp_number into the same edge slots, so once the bare edges are bucketed it stops rewarding deeper distinct-context FP-parser states and never accumulates the prefix that yields a two-dot string.
mechanism_attribution: Context-sensitive coverage feedback (naive_ctx CtxHook), not a CMP/I2S technique. naive and naive_ctx share the identical havoc+token mutator stack and SanitizerCoverage edge counters; the ONLY differing technique is naive_ctx folding a call-context hash into the edge-map index (MaxMapFeedback over a (call-context, edge) map). This finer feedback retains more distinct intermediate FP-parser states in the corpus, raising the probability that havoc mutation eventually emits a second dot. There is no comparison-logging or value-profile component to this win.

what_input_feature: An ASCII numeric string parsed by png_check_fp_number that drives the state machine all the way to PNG_FP_EXPONENT and then encounters a sign character ('+' 0x2b or '-' 0x2d) while PNG_FP_SAW_ANY is non-zero — i.e. a deep, well-formed exponent prefix like a digit run, 'E'/'e', then a sign, reaching the 'case PNG_FP_EXPONENT + PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN' arm at line 2800 with prior state bits set.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx corpus seeds drive the parser deep into the case ladder: the per-role overlay marks line 2801 as [B] and line 2802 'goto PNG_FP_End' as [W] (winner-only), and the DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows the winner takes the blocker true side T=29 while the loser never does (T=0). Reaching PNG_FP_EXPONENT+PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN with PNG_FP_SAW_ANY set requires having already consumed a sign and an exponent marker, a multi-character chain naive_ctx's context-indexed feedback rewards each step of.
why_loser_doesnt: naive seeds never reach the true side: the DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows the loser at the blocker as T=0 F=20, so it only ever enters the case arm with PNG_FP_SAW_ANY clear (or not at all). naive uses the identical mutator stack but a bare per-edge bucket feedback, so it gets no new-coverage credit for the intermediate parser states needed to assemble the deep exponent-plus-sign sequence and stalls (blocked 24.00h, hitcount 0).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same png_check_fp_number basic-block edges count as new coverage under each distinct caller chain. This rewards progressively deeper exploration of the floating-point case ladder that naive's bare per-edge map saturates on, letting naive_ctx accumulate the multi-character state sequence (digits -> E -> sign) that reaches the PNG_FP_EXPONENT+PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN arm with PNG_FP_SAW_ANY set. Both fuzzers share havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the divergence is purely the coverage-granularity feedback dimension.

what_input_feature: The same trailing zero-length-IDAT structure reaching the line-833 false branch, but the divergence here is feedback-granularity: the false side is reached when the IDAT arm executes under a call context naive's flat edge map already considers covered.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 while naive resolves 0/10; the CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the line-833 edge reached via the trailing-IDAT path is a new (call-context, edge) bucket that rewards and retains the intermediate input, letting it be mutated toward the false side.
why_loser_doesnt: naive's flat MaxMapFeedback marks the line-833 region covered the first time any IDAT path hits it, so the trailing-zero-length-IDAT variant earns no new-coverage credit and is not retained; naive stays blocked at the branch (loser T=44 F=0).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook context-indexed edge map) creates new coverage buckets for the IDAT path reached under a distinct caller context, providing the retention/scheduling pressure that plain naive's edge-only feedback never supplies; no I2S or CMP_MAP is involved.

what_input_feature: The same color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03 to enter the line 883 case body; no new comparison structure, just the same equality.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 trials and its winner-resolving seeds (the displayed naive_ctx seeds) all carry 0x03 at offset 0x19.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 9/10 with non-3 color_type bytes; the same havoc mutator stack as naive_ctx but flat edge indexing leaves it stuck.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx uses naive's identical havoc/token mutators (no I2S, no CMP_MAP), but the CtxHook folds call-context into the edge map so reaching line 883 via a new caller chain registers as new coverage; this richer corpus-retention keeps more IHDR-shaped intermediates alive, raising the odds a havoc mutation lands 0x03 at offset 0x19.

what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a gAMA chunk reached with length != 4. The feature axis distinguishing the winner here is not byte-solving but the call-context under which the gAMA handler edge is recorded as new coverage.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 with avg blocked duration only 0.90h. Its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching png_handle_gAMA via a distinct call path registers as a new (call-context, edge) bucket, retaining intermediate corpus entries that bring the fuzzer to the gAMA length comparison and let havoc flip the length field off 4.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 10/10 for the full 24.00h. With bare per-edge buckets it does not retain the context-distinct intermediates that lead to the gAMA length check, so it lacks the corpus scaffolding to mutate the length field to a non-4 value and stays on the false branch.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook installs a running call-context hash folded into the edge-map index) makes (call-context, edge) pairs count as new coverage, growing the corpus along the path to the gAMA length check; naive's plain MaxMapFeedback over bare edges does not.

what_input_feature: The same IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03; naive_ctx differs from naive only in context-sensitive edge indexing, not in any comparison-solving stage.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves in 9/10 trials (avg hitcount 39); all winner-resolving seeds shown are fuzzer=naive_ctx and carry 03 at offset 0x19, and context-indexed edges distinguish the call paths through png_handle_sBIT, retaining more intermediate corpus entries that carry the palette color_type forward.
why_loser_doesnt: plain naive folds the same edges into a single map slot regardless of call context, so context-distinct paths that preserve a near-palette IHDR are not separately rewarded, and naive stays blocked in 9/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same edge counts as new coverage when reached via a different call context; this finer feedback retains more corpus diversity (including inputs whose IHDR color_type can be mutated toward 3) but uses the identical havoc/token mutator stack as naive with no CMP introspection.

what_input_feature: Same program-side target — an 'sRGB'-typed chunk reaching png_handle_sRGB with chunk length != 1 — but reached incrementally as the input progresses through the chunk-dispatch call path.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching png_handle_sRGB through a new call context registers as new coverage and the intermediate seed is retained, letting the campaign build toward the length!=1 true side (winner resolved 10/10, branch hit 11 times in W).
why_loser_doesnt: plain naive records only bare edges, so a deeper chunk-dispatch path that re-hits already-covered edges yields no new coverage and the progressing seed is discarded; naive never retains the stepping stones and stays blocked (resolved 1/10, blocked 9/10, avg 21.80h blocked).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook makes (call-context, edge) pairs the coverage unit, so the same chunk-dispatch edge counts as new when reached via a fresh caller chain into png_handle_sRGB; this finer feedback preserves intermediate inputs that drive toward the length!=1 branch, which bare-edge naive cannot.

what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 3 so the bKGD handler reaches the palette-out-of-place body; the same edge is newly rewarded under a distinct call context.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 and its winner-resolving seeds (stored under naive_ctx) carry 08 03 at 0x18-0x19, taking the W-only out-of-place body at L1934-L1938.
why_loser_doesnt: naive (no context indexing) keeps color_type at 06/04/00, never produces 0x03, and blocks the branch in 9/10 trials with avg duration 21.60h.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching the bKGD comparison via a new call context counts as new coverage; this preserves intermediate inputs that naive discards, retaining more parent material whose havoc mutation eventually lands color_type=3 at offset 0x19.

what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG (IHDR present, no prior IDAT, no duplicate sCAL, sCAL length >= 4) that reaches png_read_buffer(png_ptr, length+1, ...) in OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_sCAL under a heap/allocation state where the buffer allocation returns NULL, so the blocked true branch at L2415 is taken. The feature is not a specific literal byte gate but reaching the sCAL handler with an allocation-failing program state.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all 10 trials (resolved=10/10, avg hitcount 14, blocked side taken T=11 in the per-role overlay). Its context-sensitive coverage records the sCAL edge under distinct call-context indices, so reaching png_handle_sCAL along varied call paths (and the allocation sub-paths inside png_read_buffer) registers as new coverage and is retained, steering the corpus toward the input/heap state that makes the allocation fail.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 9/10 trials (resolved=1/10, blocked side L2415 T=0 F=12). With plain edge coverage the bare sCAL edge is counted once regardless of call context, so once the false (buffer non-NULL) side is hit, traversing png_handle_sCAL again yields no new edge-bucket signal and the campaign is never rewarded for exploring the allocation-failure sub-state.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is a new coverage entry per call path. This (call-context, edge) granularity rewards reaching png_read_buffer / png_handle_sCAL via more contexts, driving exploration into the rarely-taken out-of-memory branch. naive's plain MaxMapFeedback over bare edges gives no such gradient. The divergence is attributable to the context-sensitive coverage technique, the single difference between the two fuzzers.

what_input_feature: Reaching the tIME-handling path (line 2490 true branch) via a particular call context, where the same edge counted under its caller chain registers as new coverage even though plain naive already saw the bare edge elsewhere.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 and the per-role overlay shows the true side at lines 2491-2495 fired as winner-only [W]; its CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the tIME-dispatch edge under png_handle_tIME's caller chain is a new (call-context, edge) bucket, giving energy to inputs progressing toward the chunk handler.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain naive blocks the branch 8/10 (T=0 F=36) and spends 19.50h blocked; its bare per-edge MaxMapFeedback does not distinguish the tIME-dispatch edge by call context, so reaching png_handle_tIME with a non-7-length chunk earns no new bucket and the path is not rewarded.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook running call-context hash folded into the edge-map slot) makes the (call-context, edge) pair leading into the tIME handler count as new coverage, rescuing the input that plain naive's context-blind edge map treats as already-covered.

what_input_feature: A zTXt chunk whose declared length field is large enough that the call buffer = png_read_buffer(png_ptr, length, 2) cannot satisfy the allocation and returns NULL, so the (buffer == NULL) condition at pngrutil.c:2634 evaluates true and control enters the out-of-memory bailout at 2635-2639.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all 10 trials (resolved=10/10, hitcount on branch=8) and takes the true side 16 times (L2634 T=16 F=0 for W), i.e. it repeatedly drives png_read_buffer down the NULL-return path; the CtxHook re-indexes each edge by call context so the png_read_buffer / handle_zTXt edges count as fresh coverage under each distinct chunk-processing call path, rewarding the rare large-length zTXt inputs that exhaust the read buffer.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 9 of 10 trials (resolved=1/10, blocked=9) and never takes the true side (L2634 T=0 F=35 for L) — its bare edge-bucket feedback marks the png_read_buffer edge covered after the first ordinary zTXt and stops rewarding inputs that push length high enough to force a NULL allocation, so it stays on the false branch and proceeds into the keyword-parsing tail (L2641, L2647-2708 hit only in L).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index (HookableInProcessExecutor), so the same png_read_buffer edge is recorded at different map slots per call path; a new (call-context, edge) pair is novel coverage that keeps the large-length / OOM zTXt inputs alive in the corpus, whereas plain naive's MaxMapFeedback over the bare edge map saturates the edge and abandons that direction. This is a coverage-granularity (feedback-dimension) advantage, not comparison solving.

what_input_feature: The same structurally-valid iTXt chunk that reaches the L2796 translated-keyword NUL-scan loop and exits it on the false side; no comparison-solving is required, only that the input region around the translated-keyword offset contains a non-zero byte before `length`.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 (avg hitcount on branch winner=6); its CtxHook indexes each edge by call-context, so the iTXt-handler edges reached via the chunk-dispatch path register as new (call-context, edge) coverage, retaining intermediate iTXt seeds that naive discards and giving energy to mutants that progress through the prefix loops to L2796.
why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 and stays blocked 9/10 (avg duration blocked loser=21.80h); its plain edge-bucket feedback already saw the iTXt loop edges from shallower chunks, so context-blind coverage gives no new-bucket reward for partially-advanced iTXt seeds and they are not retained long enough to mutate to the L2796 exit.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook folding the caller chain into the edge-map index) creates fresh coverage credit for the iTXt loop edges reached via the chunk dispatcher, preserving and energizing the intermediate seeds needed to walk prefix_length past the translated-keyword field; naive's bare-edge feedback offers no such credit.

what_input_feature: The same iTXt-dispatch-passing chunk that reaches L2810 and takes its false side, surfaced by exploring the call-context-specific edge into png_handle_iTXt rather than by solving any byte comparison.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 (blocked 1/10, avg 7.60h); the winner-resolving seeds shown are all stored by naive_ctx (fuzzer=naive_ctx, trial=1) and carry the iTXt-chunk byte layout that takes the false branch.
why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 (blocked 9/10, avg 20.30h); with a bare edge-bucket map it stops rewarding the iTXt path once the edge is seen and never accumulates corpus pressure to refine the chunk into the false-side compression-flag/length combination.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same iTXt-handler edge is a NEW (call-context, edge) coverage bucket whenever reached via a fresh call path; this extra granularity keeps rewarding and retaining iTXt seeds, giving the havoc stack many more shots at building the false-side chunk than naive's context-blind map.

what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03 to take the true branch at line 276 inside OSS_FUZZ_png_set_IHDR.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry 0x03 at offset 0x19 (W bytes 03(.)x30); naive_ctx indexes the same edges by call context, so reaching png_set_IHDR via additional call paths registers new (context,edge) coverage and rewards intermediate inputs that retain more PNG chunk structure.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds never set color_type to 3 (L bytes 00(.)x7 04(.)x2 06(.)x1); naive's flat edge map gives no extra credit for distinct call contexts, so it preserves fewer structurally-valid intermediates that could be mutated into the PALETTE value.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same edge counts as new coverage under different call contexts; this finer feedback retains more chunk-valid corpus members from which havoc reaches color_type==3, whereas naive's bare-edge map does not.

what_input_feature: Input must drive xmlFreeNode on a node whose type is XML_TEXT_NODE or XML_COMMENT_NODE, so the compound guard at tree.c:3891-3893 short-circuits false on the type check; this is reached when content-bearing text/comment nodes (with cur->content != NULL) are freed during parse/teardown rather than only element/DTD-subset nodes.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx reaches the content-freeing else-if at lines 3880-3884 ([W] 3883 DICT_FREE(cur->content)) and at the blocker line 3893 takes the false side (T=0 F=3), meaning the freed node carried content and was a text/comment node; the context hook surfaced the xmlFreeDtd subset-cleanup call path (lines 1121-1132 all [W]) as new (call-context, edge) coverage that pulled the campaign into this teardown path.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the false side: at the blocker it only ever takes true (T=18 F=0) and never hits the content-free else-if (line 3883 is [L]-absent, line 3894 DICT_FREE(cur->name) is [L]); naive's bare-edge MaxMapFeedback gives no new-coverage credit for reaching xmlFreeNode through the text/comment-node call context, so it stalls (blocked 11.70h, 0 hits) freeing only name-bearing nodes.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same xmlFreeNode edge reached via the text/comment-node teardown path is a new (call-context, edge) bucket and earns a corpus-add; naive's plain per-edge bucket already saw that edge via name-node frees and gives no reward, so only the context-sensitive feedback dimension rewards exploring the false side.

what_input_feature: Same program-side condition, but the distinguishing requirement is that xmlHashAddEntry3 is reached via multiple distinct call contexts (entity, attribute, relaxng paths) so context-indexed coverage keeps exploring the parse paths that lead to a new-triple insertion.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 and blocks 0/10; its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same xmlHashAddEntry3 edge reached from different callers (xmlAddEntity, xmlAddSpecialAttr, relaxng) is fresh coverage, keeping inputs that drive deeper distinct parse contexts toward an insertion.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive folds all call paths into the same bare edge, so re-reaching xmlHashAddEntry3 from a new caller earns no new coverage; it loses the exploration pressure on the deeper parse contexts and blocks 8/10.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook records (call-context, edge) pairs) makes each distinct caller-chain reaching xmlHashAddEntry3 new coverage, sustaining exploration of the parse paths that produce a distinct hash triple at line 580.

what_input_feature: A DTD ATTLIST enumeration that opens with '(' (passing the XML_ERR_ATTLIST_NOT_STARTED guard) but whose first token is not a valid NmToken, so xmlParseNmtoken returns NULL inside xmlParseEnumerationType and that caller raises XML_ERR_NMTOKEN_REQUIRED.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx evolves seeds carrying ATTLIST/enumeration DTD structure (e.g. '<!ATTLIST', '(b*)' constructs), and the CtxHook makes the (xmlParseEnumerationType->xmlFatalErr, NMTOKEN case) edge a distinct coverage point, so a seed driving that specific call path is retained and refined toward the true side; the winner hits the case 30/69 times.
why_loser_doesnt: naive folds all callers of xmlFatalErr into one edge index, so reaching the XML_ERR_NMTOKEN_REQUIRED case via this caller earns no new coverage over the many other error cases it already hits; the loser-blocking seeds reach the switch 286 times but never the NMTOKEN case (T=0 F=286).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same xmlFatalErr basic-block edge is recorded at distinct slots per call path; this gives the enumeration-error path its own coverage reward that bare-edge naive never grants, retaining and refining seeds toward the NMTOKEN case. This is context-sensitive coverage, not comparison-solving.

what_input_feature: A decimal character reference (`&#` followed by many ASCII digits, no terminating `;` early) so the `while (RAW != ';')` loop in xmlParseCharRef iterates more than 20 times and the count++>20 reset branch becomes a distinct (call-context, edge) coverage event.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winner-resolving seeds drive the loop deeply: the loop header L2352 is taken True 111 times and the blocker L2353 True 9 times under W, with an average branch hitcount of 57, so the count overflow side is exercised. Context-sensitive indexing records each call path into this loop as fresh coverage, keeping such seeds in the corpus.
why_loser_doesnt: naive's loser-blocking seeds enter the loop only 4 times (L2352 T=4) and never reach the count overflow (L2353 T=0), with average hitcount 1; bare edge feedback already saturates the loop edges after a few iterations so naive has no coverage incentive to keep growing the digit run.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same loop-body edge inside xmlParseCharRef is a new bucket per call context. This rewards seeds that re-enter the digit loop along new paths, pushing the iteration count up until count++>20 fires — a feedback-granularity effect, not comparison solving.

what_input_feature: An XML element/attribute QName whose prefix exceeds XML_MAX_NAMELEN so the dynamic-buffer copy loop at parser.c:3013 is entered, and which is terminated (by 0 or ':') so the loop takes its false exit rather than spinning forever on more name bytes; this is reached only through deep, well-formed enough SAX element/attribute parsing (entity refs, references, attribute processing).
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx seeds carry a recognizable XML skeleton (`\n<ml v...`, `<?xml version=`, `<ISO-10646-UCS-2`) and runs of repeated bytes (0xc0, 0xb2, 0xb5, 'x') that form an oversized name; the winner's hit-count profile shows it drives the deeper parser states the false-exit path requires (xmlParseEntityRef W=72 vs L=0, xmlParseReference W=54 vs L=0), and the blocker's false side is actually taken in the W role (L3013 F=36/F=6 with buffer present).
why_loser_doesnt: naive seeds are dominated by '.' fill (0x2e) and a `\n<Rxul...` skeleton that never builds an oversized terminated prefix and never reaches the entity/reference parsing depth (xmlParseEntityRef/xmlParseReference L hits = 0); the loser blocks the false side because the surrounding parse states are never explored, not because of any single comparand byte.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S and no CMP_MAP, so the divergence is not comparison-solving. naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller call-context hash into the edge-map index, so an edge inside xmlSplitQName reached via a NEW SAX2 call path (e.g. through xmlSAX2AttributeInternal vs xmlSAX2StartElement) registers as new coverage. That extra granularity rewards and retains the intermediate seeds that progressively build a longer prefix and deeper parse state, letting the context-sensitive arm climb to the loop's false-exit state that the bare-edge naive arm prunes as already-covered.

what_input_feature: An input that drives the parser into xmlParseCharDataComplex with a >=XML_PARSER_BIG_BUFFER_SIZE character run while ctxt->disableSAX is true (a prior fatal error has set disableSAX==1), so the guard (sax!=NULL && !disableSAX) at L4653 evaluates FALSE; the well-formed DOCTYPE-bearing XML of the winner seeds reaches this state through a richer set of caller call-contexts than the malformed loser inputs.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are well-formed XML beginning '<?xml version="1.0"?>' with a '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' header, exercising clean parser states that let naive_ctx's context-indexed feedback retain many distinct (call-context, edge) corpus entries reaching the blocker; the divergent-branch dump shows the winner does take the FALSE side (W L4653 T=0 F=2) which the loser never reaches.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are malformed byte soup (e.g. 'ht~p://-/' and long runs of repeated bytes) that stall on the TRUE side only (L L4653 T=16 F=0); plain naive's bare-edge feedback collapses the many call paths into xmlParseCharDataComplex onto the same edge slots, so it never retains a corpus path that arrives at the big-buffer flush with disableSAX==1.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx and naive share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S or CMP_MAP; the only differing technique is naive_ctx's CtxHook, which folds the caller-chain hash into the edge-map index so the same blocker edge counts as new coverage when reached through a new call context. This context granularity preserves more diverse paths into xmlParseCharDataComplex, eventually surfacing an input whose disableSAX state flips the L4653 guard FALSE.

what_input_feature: A comment body (after a <!-- opener) containing a literal 0x09 tab byte at a position not already matched by the >0x2D or 0x20..0x2C disjuncts, i.e. the byte must equal exactly 0x09.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds are naive_ctx and carry 0x09 bytes interleaved with the comment opener (e.g. seed 1 has bytes '09 09' at 0x07-0x08 and a '<!--------' run followed by '09 09 09'); context-sensitive coverage lets naive_ctx retain the intermediate seeds that built this <!--+tab structure as new (call-context, edge) coverage.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain naive shares the identical havoc/token mutator set but only a bare per-edge bucket, so the partial-progress comment seeds collapse to already-covered edges and are discarded; the loser-blocking naive seeds contain '--' runs but no 0x09 tab inside a comment body, so L4985 is taken only on its False side (T=0).
mechanism_attribution: context-sensitive coverage: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching xmlParseComment's content loop via a new call path counts as new coverage and preserves the staging seeds that bare naive throws away.

what_input_feature: A DTD <!ATTLIST ...> enumerated-type declaration that reaches the '(' of an enumeration list and then presents an empty or non-NmToken first character (so xmlParseNmtoken returns NULL at parser.c:5891), taking the true side of 'if (name == NULL)' at line 5892.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx's CtxHook indexes the same edges by call context, so the enumeration-parsing edges reached via the deep DTD chain (xmlParseDocTypeDecl -> ... -> xmlParseEnumerationType) form new (call-context, edge) coverage; this gives the scheduler a distinct reward for inputs that drive the parser into the enumeration list and corner-case it onto the name==NULL path (winner: resolved=8/10, T side hit 30 times).
why_loser_doesnt: naive uses bare per-edge bucket feedback; the edges around line 5892 are already covered by the dominant false-branch path (name!=NULL), so plain naive gets no new-coverage signal for steering into the empty-token corner and stays blocked (loser: blocked=8/10, true side hitcount 0 at line 5892).
mechanism_attribution: Context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook): folding the caller chain into the edge-map index makes the enumeration-parse edges reached through the deep DTD attribute-list call path count as new coverage, rewarding inputs that exercise the rarely-taken empty-NmToken branch that bare edge coverage cannot distinguish.

what_input_feature: Same byte-level requirement (a '-' at NXT(2) after '<!' producing '<!--'), but reached because the comment/markup-decl arm becomes a NEW coverage bucket under context-sensitive indexing since xmlParseMarkupDecl is reached from multiple call contexts.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same xmlParseMarkupDecl switch edges register as distinct (call-context, edge) pairs across the internal-subset / external-subset / conditional-section call paths; this rewards exploring the markup-decl switch arms and drives the corpus toward inputs that exercise case '-'.
why_loser_doesnt: naive uses a bare per-edge map, so once the switch edges are hit via any one call path they are no longer 'new', removing the gradient that would push exploration toward the case '-' arm; it stays blocked all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook makes (call-context, edge) pairs count as new coverage, so the multi-caller xmlParseMarkupDecl switch arms each yield fresh feedback; this context-sensitive coverage granularity is what distinguishes naive_ctx from naive.

what_input_feature: The input must reach xmlCurrentChar with a 3-byte UTF-8 lead byte (c & 0xf0 == 0xe0) followed by two valid continuation bytes (each (b & 0xc0)==0x80) such that the decoded value (cur0&0xf)<<12 | (cur1&0x3f)<<6 | (cur2&0x3f) is below 0x800 — i.e. an overlong 3-byte encoding that triggers the goto encoding_error true side at line 622.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in 8/10 trials and actually executes the true side (W shows T=42 F=0 at line 622), reaching the 3-byte decode block (lines 617-624 marked [B] in the per-role overlay). Context-sensitive coverage indexes the same xmlCurrentChar edge by its distinct call context (e.g. the xmlParseCharDataComplex -> xmlCurrentChar path), so an input that lands a 3-byte sequence in a never-before-seen caller chain registers as new coverage and is retained, letting energy accumulate on the overlong-3-byte case.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 8/10 trials and never takes the true side (L shows T=0 F=1730 at line 622): it produces many 3-byte sequences but they all decode to >= 0x800, so they hit the same flat edge and never get extra retention. Without context indexing the overlong-3-byte input is not distinguished from the common valid-3-byte input, so naive lacks the corpus pressure to refine bytes toward val < 0x800.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the xmlCurrentChar 3-byte decode edge counts as new coverage when reached via a new call context; this extra granularity retains intermediate seeds that naive's bare-edge feedback discards, giving the schedule the foothold to drive the decoded value below 0x800. This is context-sensitive coverage, not comparison-solving.

what_input_feature: An XML input that reaches xmlNewNs through the SAX2 attribute-internal call context (xmlSAX2AttributeInternal -> xmlNewNs at SAX2.c:1214), where the call path itself is the distinguishing signal.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching the xmlNewNs edge via the SAX2 namespace-declaration call context counts as new (call-context, edge) coverage; this rewards inputs progressing down the attribute path and lifts branch hitcount to 79 with 8/10 resolved.
why_loser_doesnt: plain naive records only the bare edge, so once any path has touched the xmlNewNs region it earns no further credit for reaching it through the namespace-declaration context, leaving the path under-explored — naive resolves only 2/10 with hitcount 4.
mechanism_attribution: context-sensitive coverage: naive_ctx's CtxHook keeps a running hash of the caller chain and indexes the edge map by (call-context, edge), so the SAX2-attribute call path into xmlNewNs is treated as fresh coverage and explored, whereas naive collapses it to one already-seen edge.

what_input_feature: The same default-namespace `xmlns="..."` attribute reaching xmlNewNs(ctxt->node, val, NULL); the distinguishing feature is that xmlNewNs is reached via a particular SAX call context (xmlSAX2AttributeInternal at SAX2.c:1214) that context-sensitive coverage treats as new.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching xmlNewNs through the default-namespace call path (xmlSAX2AttributeInternal at line 1214) is a new (call-context, edge) pair that gets preserved as corpus; this keeps inputs that drive the attribute path alive and it resolves the false branch (8/10, hitcount 79 vs 4).
why_loser_doesnt: naive's plain edge-bucket feedback gives no extra credit for reaching xmlNewNs through the namespaced-attribute caller versus other callers, so the intermediate inputs that almost reach the call site are not retained and it stays blocked (8/10, hitcount 4).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook (call-context, edge) pairs) rewards reaching xmlNewNs through the default-namespace SAX caller as new coverage, retaining the path that bare naive discards.

what_input_feature: The same parsed-namespace-with-prefix condition driving xmlFreeNs's false side, with the divergence attributable to which call-context reaches the shared parsing/free edges rather than to comparison solving.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 79 (vs naive's 4); its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same edges reached via the xmlTextReaderFreeNode/xmlFreeNsList call path register as new (call-context, edge) coverage and keep seeds that drive deeper into the reader's node-freeing path.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 with branch hitcount 4; with bare edge coverage it saturates the shared edges quickly and stops rewarding inputs that reach xmlFreeNs through the distinct reader call contexts visible in the loser-blocking d=3 xmlTextReaderRead / d=2 xmlTextReaderFreeNode chain.
mechanism_attribution: context-sensitive coverage: naive_ctx's CtxHook indexes edges by call-context hash so (call-context, edge) pairs count as new coverage, preserving seeds that exercise the namespace-free path via the xmlTextReader call chain that naive's plain edge feedback treats as already-covered.

what_input_feature: The same default-namespace nsDef lookup, but reached via xmlSearchNs invoked from the element-start call context where ctxt's namespace stack makes prefix==NULL/nameSpace==NULL the live case.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 144 vs naive's 8; its context-indexed edge map records xmlSearchNs edges separately per call path (e.g. the xmlSAX2StartElement caller at SAX2.c:1737), so reaching the blocker under the namespace-search context is rewarded as new coverage and that input is preserved.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (L6182 T=0 F=180); with a bare edge map the xmlSearchNs edge is already covered from some other call path, so an input that first reaches the prefix==NULL/nameSpace==NULL case is not seen as new coverage and is not retained.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the (call-context, edge) pair at the xmlSearchNs namespace lookup is novel coverage that naive's bare edge counter cannot distinguish, retaining the input that exercises the default-namespace path.

what_input_feature: The input must contain a processing instruction emitted while the parser is inside the internal DTD subset, i.e. a `<?...?>` between `<!DOCTYPE name [` and the closing `]>`, so that ctxt->inSubset == 1 when xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction runs and the true branch at L2740 is taken (then xmlAddChild to intSubset at L2741).
why_winner_satisfies: The naive_ctx winner resolves 8/10 and takes the true side (L2740 T=2 in W vs T=0 in L); its seed carries a well-formed `<?xml version="1.0"?>` prolog plus a `<!DOCTYPE a...` declaration, the structural scaffold needed to enter inSubset==1 when a PI is parsed inside the subset. Context-sensitive coverage rewards the PI handler reached via the DTD-internal-subset call context as a distinct new edge, so the path into the subset PI handler stays in the corpus and gets driven to the inSubset==1 site.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 10/10 and only ever takes the false side (L2740 T=0 F=91 in L); it reaches xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction 91 times but with inSubset==0, so it falls through to the parent/root handling at L2747-L2755 (the [L] lines). Its inputs have the DOCTYPE/PI fragments mangled and never assemble a PI nested inside an open internal subset, so the inSubset==1 state never co-occurs with a PI parse.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between naive_ctx and naive is the CtxHook: naive_ctx folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction via the internal-subset parse path is a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket that naive's bare-edge map cannot distinguish from the much more frequent root-level PI path. This context-sensitive coverage keeps the subset-PI input alive in the corpus and steers mutation toward the inSubset==1 true branch; the mutators are identical havoc/token (no I2S, no CMP_MAP).

what_input_feature: Input must contain a comment opener `<!--` (bytes 3c 21 2d 2d) that runs into a short, unterminated comment so that in xmlParseCommentComplex the third decoded char `cur = CUR_CHAR(l)` is 0 (end of input), forcing `if (cur == 0)` true and `goto not_terminated`.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry `<!--` followed by long runs of `-` dashes and truncated tails (e.g. seed bytes `3c 21 2d 2d 2d ...`), and the per-role overlay marks line 4847 `goto not_terminated` and the not_terminated label (4920-4924) as winner-only [W], showing the winner repeatedly hits the cur==0 true side (W T=23 at L4846).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry DOCTYPE/DTD-heavy structure (e.g. `DOCTYPE`, `.dtd`, `<!ELEM`) so their comments terminate or never reach the third-char-EOF state; at the blocker L4846 the loser shows T=0 F=40, never taking the cur==0 true side.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the xmlParseCommentComplex cur==0 edge is recorded as a distinct (call-context, edge) bucket reached only via the comment-parse path; this new-context coverage rewards seeds that drive an unterminated comment to EOF, retaining and refining them, whereas naive collapses the same edge into one already-seen bucket and never schedules energy toward the truncated-comment state.

what_input_feature: An XML input whose <!DOCTYPE ...> internal subset declares at least one DTD member (element decl, attribute decl, entity, notation, or parameter entity), so dtd->elements (etc.) is non-NULL and the line-686 conjunction is false, taking the ' [\n' body-dump branch rather than the bare '>' close.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winning seeds carry DOCTYPE-with-content structure: seed 2/3 contain '<!DOCTYPE a S[<!ELEMENT' and the W role uniquely executes xmlBufDumpElementDecl (W=10, L=0), i.e. an element declaration was parsed into dtd->elements; on the blocker the winner takes the false (body) side (L686 W: T=0 F=10) and proceeds into the children loop (L706 W: T=12 F=10).
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds reach the DTD node but stay on the empty-DTD true side (L686 L: T=10 F=0) and never enter the body; the loser never invokes xmlBufDumpElementDecl (L=0) and instead exercises the SYSTEM-id path / escaping functions, meaning its parsed DTD has all five member tables NULL.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S and no CMP_MAP; the only difference is naive_ctx's CtxHook, which folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so that reaching the line-686 edge via the XML_DTD_NODE dispatch in xmlNodeDumpOutputInternal is recorded as a NEW (call-context, edge) bucket. This context-sensitive coverage rewards the intermediate states along the DTD-population path, giving the corpus the structural stepping-stones to build a non-empty-subset DOCTYPE, whereas bare-edge naive collapses those distinct contexts onto already-covered edges and gets no new-coverage signal to retain them.
